text.title: Condensed Spec Format and Authentication Example
text.summary: The five minimum sections of a condensed spec and a complete authentication example demonstrating each section at MVP scope.

rule.statement: A condensed spec must include exactly these five sections: Problem Statement, User Stories (max 3), Functional Requirements (Must only), Acceptance Criteria (1 happy path + 1 error case), Out of Scope.
rule.rationale: These five sections preserve traceability (FR traces to US, AC validates FR) while eliminating overhead that adds no new information at MVP scope.

rule.statement: Write Out of Scope immediately after User Stories — before writing FRs. This prevents scope creep from accumulating before requirements are defined.
rule.rationale: Out of Scope written last tends to be incomplete. Written second, it explicitly bounds what the FRs must NOT address.

section.title: Token Budget
section.p: Typical condensed spec size by section:

rule.statement: A condensed spec must stay under 80 lines. Use wc -l as a quick size check before committing.
rule.rationale: Condensed specs that grow past 80 lines are no longer condensed — they have absorbed full-spec content and should be upgraded to spec-structure v2.0.

example.note: Complete condensed spec for User Authentication (cashflow-planner MVP)
example.code:
# Feature: User Authentication
**Version:** 1.0 **Status:** Approved **Project:** cashflow-planner
## Reference Documents | Document | Path | |----------|------| | Constitution | `.aidocs/constitution.md` |
## Problem Statement **Who:** New users of the cashflow planning app **Problem:** Cannot access personal financial data because there is no authentication **Impact:** Users cannot save cashflow projections, blocking core value proposition **Solution:** Email-based authentication with JWT **Success Metric:** 1000 registered users in first month
## User Stories
### US-001: Email Registration (MVP) **As a** financial planner **I want to** create an account with my email **So that** I can save and access my cashflow projections **Priority:** Must **Acceptance Criteria:** AC-001, AC-002
## Functional Requirements
| ID | Requirement | Traces To | Priority | |----|-------------|-----------|----------| | FR-001 | System SHALL allow registration with email + password | US-001 | Must | | FR-002 | System SHALL validate email format (RFC 5322) | US-001 | Must | | FR-003 | System SHALL require password min 8 chars, 1 upper, 1 number | US-001 | Must |
## Acceptance Criteria
### AC-001: Successful Registration **Given:** User is on registration page **When:** User enters valid email and valid password **Then:** Account is created **And:** User receives verification email within 30 seconds **And:** User is redirected to dashboard
### AC-002: Registration with Existing Email **Given:** Email "test@example.com" exists in system **When:** User tries to register with "test@example.com" **Then:** Error message "Email already registered" is displayed **And:** No duplicate account is created
## Out of Scope
| Feature | Reason | When | |---------|--------|------| | Social login | Not MVP | Phase 2 | | 2FA | Complexity | Phase 3 |
